commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The forecast strip could benefit from a slightly clearer visual grouping (e.g., individual small cards or pill backgrounds) for better readability.
issues.fix: Wrap forecast items in subtle hover-enabled background boxes.

issues.description: Icons in the forecast strip lack descriptive aria-labels, potentially rendering them meaningless to screen reader users.
issues.fix: Add 'aria-label' props to the Icon components based on the condition description.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses props effectively, and maintains a clean layout that adheres to the provided design system. Adding aria-labels to the icons and minor visual grouping for the forecast strip would elevate this to a production-ready standard.

issues.description: The wind speed and humidity section lacks clear visual separation or icons to improve scanability.
issues.fix: Add small icons before the 'Humidity' and 'Wind' labels for a more professional UI.

issues.description: The unit conversion does not update the forecast temperatures, leading to a potential inconsistency where the current temp is in F and the forecast remains in C.
issues.fix: Implement a helper function to convert the high/low forecast values based on the active unit state.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system primitives effectively, and adheres strictly to the provided contract. It provides a clean, professional look, though minor functional enhancements like unit-aware forecast conversion and icon usage in details would push it to 'production-ready'.

issues.description: The wind speed and humidity section lacks clear visual separation or icons to improve scanability.
issues.fix: Add small icons before the 'Humidity' and 'Wind' labels for a more professional UI.

issues.description: The unit conversion does not update the forecast temperatures, leading to a potential inconsistency where the current temp is in F and the forecast remains in C.
issues.fix: Implement a helper function to convert the high/low forecast values based on the active unit state.

evaluation.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system primitives effectively, and adheres strictly to the provided contract. It provides a clean, professional look, though minor functional enhancements like unit-aware forecast conversion and icon usage in details would push it to 'production-ready'.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: The 'Move' select dropdown inside the card is visually heavy and takes up significant space, cluttering the card UI.
issues.fix: Use a smaller icon button or a contextual menu/popover for moving tasks to keep cards clean.

issues.description: Task editing (as requested in the prompt) is missing; currently, there is only 'Create' and 'Move'.
issues.fix: Add an onClick/double-click handler on the task title or an edit icon button to trigger an edit state.

issues.description: The 'Add Task' form inputs lack explicit focus management when the form is opened.
issues.fix: Use a ref or autoFocus attribute on the Task Title input when isAdding becomes true.

evaluations.critique: The board is well-structured and uses design tokens effectively to create a clean, functional layout. The implementation of columns and the move logic is robust, though the card interface could be refined to better support the 'edit' requirement and cleaner interactive controls.

issues.description: Task title editing functionality is missing from the task cards despite being requested.
issues.fix: Implement an 'edit' button or toggle on double-click for the task title to allow updating existing tasks.

issues.description: Task board lacks ARIA landmarks and proper heading hierarchy structure within cards.
issues.fix: Use section tags for columns and ensure interactive elements have meaningful labels, especially for the move dropdown.

issues.description: The 'Add Task' form causes layout shift and styling inconsistency with the existing cards.
issues.fix: Wrap the form in a persistent card-like container to prevent jumping, or use a Modal for the creation process.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional and well-structured, successfully implementing the requested Kanban logic and data management. It needs minor refinements to fulfill all UX requirements (specifically the title edit feature) and improvements to semantic structure for better accessibility.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The grid alignment is tight, and the card styles don't account for the gaps in the periodic table (lanthanides/actinides often require specific layout handling).
issues.fix: Add empty grid cells or logic to handle the separation of lanthanide/actinide series to match standard periodic table layouts.

issues.description: Clicking an element doesn't provide visual feedback that it is currently active, and the search filter hides elements completely via opacity, making the remaining ones look disconnected.
issues.fix: Add a 'selected' border or ring around the clicked element and improve the filtered state transition.

issues.description: Elements in the grid are missing ARIA labels describing them (e.g., 'Element Hydrogen, atomic number 1'). Keyboard users cannot navigate the grid using arrows.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the Card components and implement arrow key navigation for the grid cells.

evaluations.critique: The implementation is clean and functional, correctly leveraging the design system primitives. It meets the core requirements well, though it lacks the specific layout nuances of a real periodic table and needs better support for keyboard interaction to be truly 'interactive'.

issues.description: The periodic table layout might overflow on smaller screens due to the 18-column grid constant.
issues.fix: Wrap the grid in an overflow-x-auto container or adjust the grid-template-columns to scale based on viewport size.

issues.description: The selected element details do not automatically focus when the detail panel appears, forcing screen reader users to manually locate the content.
issues.fix: Add a ref to the detail card and call element.focus() inside a useEffect when the selected state changes.

issues.description: Visual contrast of text on category-colored backgrounds could be tight depending on the specific token color values.
issues.fix: Ensure foreground colors dynamically switch between white and black based on the background color's luminosity, or ensure all category tokens have sufficient contrast ratios.

evaluations.critique: The implementation is robust and follows the requirements accurately, using the Grid layout and design system tokens effectively. The code is clean and modular. Adding a scroll container for the grid and managing focus for the detail panel would bring this to a production-ready standard.

issues.description: The periodic table layout might overflow on smaller screens due to the 18-column grid constant.
issues.fix: Wrap the grid in an overflow-x-auto container or adjust the grid-template-columns to scale based on viewport size.

issues.description: The selected element details do not automatically focus when the detail panel appears, forcing screen reader users to manually locate the content.
issues.fix: Add a ref to the detail card and call element.focus() inside a useEffect when the selected state changes.

issues.description: Visual contrast of text on category-colored backgrounds could be tight depending on the specific token color values.
issues.fix: Ensure foreground colors dynamically switch between white and black based on the background color's luminosity, or ensure all category tokens have sufficient contrast ratios.

evaluation.critique: The implementation is robust and follows the requirements accurately, using the Grid layout and design system tokens effectively. The code is clean and modular. Adding a scroll container for the grid and managing focus for the detail panel would bring this to a production-ready standard.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

example.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
example.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

addToCart.label: Add to Cart
addToCart.description: Called when user clicks Add to Cart. Payload: { productId, quantity }

product.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
product.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

reviews.author: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Great sound quality. A bit tight for my head but breaks in after a week.

reviews.author: James P.
reviews.text: Battery life is exactly as advertised. Very impressed.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The Review step (Step 3) is missing the avatar, email notification preference, and theme preference in the summary display.
issues.fix: Add the remaining fields (avatar, emailNotifications, theme) to the Review step display.

issues.description: The avatar selection buttons are presented as generic buttons; they lack descriptive labels indicating they are for avatar selection.
issues.fix: Add 'aria-label' or screen-reader-only text to the avatar selection buttons.

issues.description: The layout is functional but lacks a clear distinction between the form content and the navigation footer in the summary step.
issues.fix: Add a Divider component before the navigation buttons to visually separate content from actions.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, clean, and fulfills the core logic requirements effectively using the provided design system. It would benefit from a more comprehensive review summary in the final step and better accessibility labels on the emoji-based avatar buttons.

issues.description: The 'Review' summary screen feels a bit visually cramped and relies heavily on repeat boilerplate code for each field.
issues.fix: Consider using a DetailList component or a more structured Grid layout to display the review data for better readability.

issues.description: The avatar selection buttons use aria-label, but there is no explicit role or grouping to indicate this is a selection set.
issues.fix: Wrap the avatar buttons in a div with role='radiogroup' and provide an aria-label='Avatar selection' to provide better context for screen readers.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly functional and cleanly implemented, effectively using the provided design system primitives. It handles state management and multi-step navigation robustly. The visual hierarchy is clear, and the inclusion of validation ensures a good user experience throughout the wizard flow.
